SEARCH
Package org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.tick

Class TickDuration

java.lang.Object
org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.tick.TickDuration
@AvailableSince("5.7") public final class TickDuration extends Object
Represents a duration in Minecraft game ticks.

This class wraps a Duration and provides convenient conversion methods between game ticks (50ms per tick), game days (24,000 ticks), and other time units. Game time is mapped to real-world seconds via: realSeconds = (ticks * 3.6) + 6*3600 (mod 24h), which means 00:00 maps to 06:00 in-game time.

Since:
5.7

  • Method Details

    • parseString

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public static io.vavr.control.Try<TickDuration> parseString(@NotNull @NotNull String timeString)
      Parses a time string into a TickDuration.
      Formats supported:
      • "123" - raw tick count
      • "10s" - seconds
      • "5d" - game days
      Parameters:
      timeString - the time string to parse
      Returns:
      a Try containing the parsed TickDuration, or a failure if parsing fails
      Since:
      5.7

    • ofDuration

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Contract(value="_ -> new", pure=true) @NotNull public static @NotNull TickDuration ofDuration(@NotNull @NotNull Duration duration)
      Creates a TickDuration from a Duration.
      Parameters:
      duration - the duration
      Returns:
      a new TickDuration
      Since:
      5.7

    • ofSeconds

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Contract(value="_ -> new", pure=true) @NotNull public static @NotNull TickDuration ofSeconds(@NotNull @NotNull Duration duration)
      Creates a TickDuration from seconds (as a Duration).
      Parameters:
      duration - the duration representing seconds
      Returns:
      a new TickDuration
      Since:
      5.7

    • ofTicks

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Contract(value="_ -> new", pure=true) @NotNull public static @NotNull TickDuration ofTicks(long ticks)
      Creates a TickDuration from a tick count.
      Parameters:
      ticks - the number of game ticks
      Returns:
      a new TickDuration
      Since:
      5.7

    • ofGameDays

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Contract(value="_ -> new", pure=true) @NotNull public static @NotNull TickDuration ofGameDays(long gameDays)
      Creates a TickDuration from a game day count.
      Parameters:
      gameDays - the number of game days (24,000 ticks each)
      Returns:
      a new TickDuration
      Since:
      5.7

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Contract(value="_, _ -> new", pure=true) @NotNull public static @NotNull TickDuration of(long time, @NotNull @NotNull TemporalUnit unit)
      Creates a TickDuration from a time value and TemporalUnit.
      Parameters:
      time - the time value
      unit - the temporal unit
      Returns:
      a new TickDuration
      Since:
      5.7

    • toLocalTime

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull public @NotNull LocalTime toLocalTime()
      Converts this duration to Minecraft local time.

      Maps the duration to real-world seconds using the formula: realSeconds = (ticks * 3.6) + 6*3600 (mod 24h)

      Returns:
      the LocalTime in Minecraft time (where 00:00 = 06:00 in-game)
      Since:
      5.7

    • toTicks

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public long toTicks()
      Converts this duration to game ticks.
      Returns:
      the number of game ticks (1 tick = 50ms)
      Since:
      5.7

    • toGameDays

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public double toGameDays()
      Converts this duration to game days.
      Returns:
      the number of game days (1 day = 24,000 ticks)
      Since:
      5.7

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public double toSeconds()
      Converts this duration to seconds.
      Returns:
      the duration in seconds
      Since:
      5.7

      Returns:
      the duration in the specified unit

      Returns:

    • isExactTo

      @AvailableSince("5.7") public boolean isExactTo(@NotNull @NotNull TimeUnit unit)
      Checks whether this duration is an exact multiple of the given TimeUnit.
      Parameters:
      unit - the time unit to test against
      Returns:
      true if this duration divides evenly by the unit, otherwise false
      Since:
      5.7

    • isExactTo
